title: Estimators, valuers and assessors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Plan, direct, or coordinate accounting, investing, banking, insurance, securities, and other financial activities of a branch, office, or department of an establishment. Direct financial activities, such as planning, procurement, and investments for all or part of an organization. (Based on 46 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Explain real and personal property taxes to property owners, and provide assessments for taxed properties, including details of taxability and assessed values.
Contact or interview claimants, doctors, medical specialists, or employers to obtain additional information to support property valuation.
Inspect personal or business property, examining factors such as market value, condition, and building or replacement costs to determine appraisal value.
Document and photograph physical characteristics of properties, including measurements, quality, design, and structural details, for appraisal reports.

Forecast property values, calculate value based on comparisons to recent sales, estimated costs, and anticipated income streams, and compute final estimations taking into account depreciation and other factors.
Determine the type of valuation necessary based on the property owner's needs and circumstances, and document findings in written appraisal reports.
Evaluate land and neighborhoods, considering market trends or impending changes, and maintain familiarity with local real estate markets to inform property evaluations.
Inspect new construction and improvements to existing structures to assess and determine values, and conduct regular reviews of properties to identify changes.
Prepare insurance forms and reports, including repair cost estimates and recommendations, to support claims and ensure accurate assessments.
Locate and record data on sales of comparable properties, and analyze this information alongside surrounding area evaluations to support valuation recommendations.
Review and verify repair cost estimates with repair shops to ensure agreement on property appraisals and adjust estimates based on findings.
Review information regarding property transfers for accuracy and ensure compliance with building codes and zoning bylaws during property appraisals.
Collect and analyze relevant data and evidence to support contested claims in court, and testify in court regarding property values and appraisals as needed.
Interview individuals familiar with the properties and surrounding areas, such as contractors, homeowners, and realtors, to gather pertinent information related to appraisals.
Supervise claims adjusters and ensure compliance with appraisal methods and processes to maintain professional standards in property evaluations.
Maintain and update accurate records and databases of appraisals, including completed appraisals and ongoing claims, for reference and compliance.
Analyze market data, transactions, and income records to determine property values and ensure assessments are fair and accurate.
Prepare, submit, and review appraisal reports for various types of property, including specialized items like jewelry and equipment, ensuring compliance with standards.
Provide direction and assistance to other organizational units regarding policies, procedures, and financial resources related to property evaluation.
Advise management on financial objectives and strategies related to property valuation and organizational resource utilization.
Establish protocols for assessing property of various classes and maintain uniform systems for appraisal evaluations across different categories.
Assess and interpret legal descriptions of properties against county records for accuracy and compliance during the appraisal process.
Collect samples of air, water, gases, or solids to determine radioactivity levels of contamination and monitor personnel to determine the amounts and intensities of radiation exposure.
Identify and recommend appropriate decontamination procedures based on the size and nature of equipment and the degree of contamination.
Educate the public about fire safety and prevention, and administer regulations regarding sanitation, fire prevention, violation corrections, and related forest regulations.
Inspect areas for fire hazards, recommend prevention measures, and restrict public access during critical fire seasons.
Compile and evaluate statistical data to determine and maintain quality and reliability of products, and maintain accurate record or batch-record documentation.
Prepare environmental assessment reports, including tabulating data and preparing charts or graphs, and provide initial response to abnormal events or alarms from radiation monitoring equipment.
Monitor and test decontamination methods and equipment performance to ensure compliance with design specifications and safety standards.

Estimate sizes and characteristics of fires, and report findings to base camps by radio or telephone. Relay messages about emergencies, accidents, locations of crew and personnel, and fire hazard conditions. Direct crews working on firelines during forest fires.

Monitor equipment during operation to ensure adherence to specifications for characteristics such as pressure, temperature, or flow. Verify that equipment is being operated and maintained according to quality assurance standards by observing worker performance. Maintain work area according to cleanroom or other processing standards. Inspect or test parts to determine nature or cause of defects or malfunctions. Inspect, diagnose, maintain, and operate test setups and equipment to detect malfunctions. Make repairs to robots or peripheral equipment, such as replacement of defective circuit boards, sensors, controllers, encoders, or servomotors.

Prepare detailed verbal or written presentations for scientists, engineers, project managers, or upper management. Document test results, using cameras, spreadsheets, documents, or other tools. Write technical reports or documentation, such as deviation reports, testing protocols, and trend analyses. Write or revise standard quality control operating procedures. Supply quality control data necessary for regulatory submissions. Complete documentation needed to support testing procedures, including data capture forms, equipment logbooks, or inventory forms.

Collect or compile nanotechnology research or engineering data. Analyze samples, such as air or water samples, for contaminants or other elements. Analyze performance of vehicles or components that have been redesigned to increase fuel efficiency, such as camless or dual-clutch engines or alternative types of air-conditioning systems. Analyze, estimate, or report production costs. Analyze test data for automotive systems, subsystems, or component parts. Analyze test results in relation to design or rated specifications and test objectives, and modify or adjust equipment to meet specifications. Identify quality problems and recommend solutions. Investigate or report questionable test results. Analyze engineering designs of logic or digital circuitry, motor controls, instrumentation, or data acquisition for implementation into new or existing automated, servomechanical, or other electromechanical systems.

Install or program computer hardware or machine or instrumentation software in microprocessor-based systems. Modify, maintain, or repair electrical, electronic, or mechanical components, equipment, or systems to ensure proper functioning. Design specialized or customized equipment, machines, or structures. Document test procedures and results, numerical and graphical data, and recommendations for changes in product or test methods.
Establish and maintain inventory, records, or documentation systems. Receive and inspect raw materials. Prepare graphs, charts, diagrams, or other aids to illustrate observations or conclusions.

Ensure that lab cleanliness and safety standards are maintained. Analyze, estimate, or report production costs. Prepare specifications, designs, or sketches for machines, components, or systems related to the generation, transmission, or use of mechanical or fluid energy. Prepare layouts of machinery, tools, plants, or equipment.

Monitor testing procedures to ensure that all tests are performed according to established item specifications, standard test methods, or protocols. Participate in out-of-specification and failure investigations and recommend corrective actions. Recommend tests or testing conditions in accordance with designs, customer requirements, or industry standards to ensure test validity. Monitor hazardous waste cleanup procedures to ensure proper application of nanocomposites or accomplishment of objectives.

Collaborate with scientists or engineers to design or conduct experiments for the development of nanotechnology materials, components, devices, or systems. Provide advice or training to other technicians. Assist engineers in developing, building, or testing prototypes or new products, processes, or procedures.

Perform or execute manual or automated tests of automotive system or component performance, efficiency, or durability. Test machines, components, materials, or products to determine characteristics such as performance, strength, or response to stress. Conduct failure analyses, document results, and recommend corrective actions. Test performance of vehicles that use alternative fuels, such as alcohol blends, natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, biodiesel, nano diesel, or alternative power methods, such as solar energy or hydrogen fuel cells. Analyze engineering designs of logic or digital circuitry, motor controls, instrumentation, or data acquisition for implementation into new or existing automated, servomechanical, or other electromechanical systems.

Fabricate or assemble mechanical, electrical, or electronic components or assemblies. Calibrate nanotechnology equipment, such as weighing, testing, or production equipment. Repair nanotechnology processing or testing equipment or submit work orders for equipment repair.
Gather information to assist with estimating the cost of production, including materials and labor. Conduct analyses of inventory to optimize item quantities.

Adhere to all applicable regulations, policies, and procedures for health, safety, and environmental compliance. Maintain or adjust inventory quantities based on project and item requirements. Identify and implement best practices for inventory control and tracking.
Analyze energy requirements and distribution systems to maximize the use of intermittent or inflexible renewable energy sources, such as wind or nuclear.
Calibrate, validate, or maintain laboratory equipment, and identify and troubleshoot equipment problems.
Compile laboratory test data and perform appropriate analyses, and conduct routine and non-routine analyses of materials or samples.
Maintain records of survey data and document methods used, writing technical reports containing collected information.
Evaluate new technologies and methods, and make recommendations regarding their use.
Train customers or personnel to install, use, or maintain robotic systems, and assist engineers in their design and application.
Maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems, including installation, programming, and repair of programmable controllers and robots.
Prepare documentation or presentations, including charts or graphs, and prepare project plans for equipment or facility improvements, including timelines and budgetary estimates.
Test performance of robotic assemblies and systems using various instruments, and perform visual inspections to ensure safety and reliability.
Inspect installation sites and monitor raw data quality during collection, correcting equipment as necessary.
Generate and review mechanical design documents for parts or assemblies, and oversee the work of contractors according to project requirements.
Conduct internal assessments and audits, and initiate corrective actions or order plant shutdowns in emergencies.
Cooperate with manufacturing or quality assurance staff to prepare product specifications or safety sheets, and maintain knowledge of current policies, regulations, and industrial processes.
Evaluate fire safety and prevention measures, and study the relationships between ignition sources and materials.
Analyze existing development or manufacturing procedures and suggest improvements, and conduct or coordinate training in safety laws and regulations.
Develop training materials and conduct sessions for safety protocols, and communicate with suppliers regarding equipment specifications.
Conduct research to evaluate the safety levels for products and investigate industrial accidents to determine causes and preventive measures.
Collaborate with research personnel to ensure compatibility of design and production, and advise manufacturing staff on process issues.
Develop methodologies for transferring procedures or processes from laboratories to commercial-scale, and apply automated solutions to material transfers.
Conduct research into robotic technology and develop new systems or capabilities.
Design or conduct follow-up experimentation based on data to meet objectives, and recommend procedures for detecting hazards.
Conduct analyses that address reliability or yield improvement, while maintaining technical project files for documentation.
Design engineering systems for the automation of industrial tasks, including designing robotic systems for various applications, such as material handling and manufacturing.
Conduct and oversee testing and research related to health hazards, safety regulations, and environmental compliance, including air quality and fire safety assessments.
Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of energy systems, including performing energy audits, analyzing energy consumption, and recommending energy savings strategies.
Create and maintain documentation for safety regulations, energy systems, and engineering designs, including technical reports, specifications, and standards.
Provide technical guidance or support on safety and health-related problems, including training personnel or clients on safe practices and new technologies.
Design and develop safety equipment, emissions control systems, or energy systems to enhance environmental protection or compliance with regulations.
Inspect and review plans for compliance with industry standards, including verifying safety measures and environmental impact measures in construction and installations.
Analyze the performance of solar energy systems, including developing specifications, conducting research, and ensuring project adherence to timelines and budgets.
Develop and validate engineering designs for renewable energy technologies, including wind, solar, and nanotechnology applications, focusing on efficiency and sustainability.

Conduct inspections and compliance assessments for projects to ensure adherence to regulations in energy systems, environmental health, and safety configurations, including wind and solar technologies.
Manage projects involving energy efficiency and safety, encompassing the coordination of various teams, drafting project proposals, and managing budgets.

Provide scientific or technical guidance or expertise to scientists, engineers, technologists, technicians, or others, using knowledge of chemical, analytical, or biological processes as applied to micro and nanoscale systems.
Monitor inventory levels and order supplies as necessary.
Conduct non-destructive testing to locate defects in various materials using techniques such as liquid penetrant, ultrasonic testing, thermal or infrared testing, and radiographic imaging.
Interpret or evaluate test results in accordance with applicable codes, standards, specifications, or procedures, and document non-destructive testing methods, processes, or results.
Supervise or direct the work of non-destructive testing trainees or staff, and assist engineers or scientists in the development of new products, fixtures, tools, or processes.
Maintain clean working environments according to clean room standards, and document procedures such as the calibration of optical or fiber optic equipment.
Set up or operate prototype or test apparatus and processing equipment such as lasers, recording equipment, and optical spectrum analyzers, and compute or record test data.
Develop or utilize new non-destructive testing methods, and produce images of objects on film using radiographic techniques.
Provide recommendations for optical or optic equipment design or material changes to reduce costs or processing times.
